prompts.title: Team Strategy Analysis (BGMI Squad)
prompts.prompt: Act as a senior esports analyst with 6 years scouting BGMI / Free Fire teams. Analyse a 4-player BGMI squad based on 30 recent matches I'll paste data for. Provide: (1) role fit (IGL, support, fragger, scout), (2) drop-pattern strengths + weaknesses, (3) rotation analysis (zone read), (4) gunfight win-rate by range (close, mid, long), (5) survival vs kill ratio, (6) team-chemistry indicators (revives, wallbangs, callouts), (7) opponent-matchup intel for 5 top Indian squads (S8UL, GodLike, OR, TSM India, Velocity), (8) 6-week improvement plan. Constraints: align with ESL India + BGMS framework; data-anchored; respect player privacy when sharing publicly.

prompts.title: Sponsorship Pitch to Brand Sponsor
prompts.prompt: Act as an esports business manager. Draft a 1-page sponsorship pitch to Razer / JBL / AMD India for a BGMI squad with 3.2M cumulative social following + recent 2nd place at ESL India Championship. Pitch covers: (1) team identity + brand fit, (2) audience demographic (18-25, tier-1+2 Indian, 86% male), (3) deliverables (jersey logo, social mentions, in-stream banner, content collabs), (4) pricing ₹85L for season, (5) measurement (impressions, brand recall study, redemption code conversions), (6) past sponsor case study, (7) signing bonus + bonuses tied to performance, (8) ASCI #ad disclosure compliance. Constraints: realistic INR; honest reach metrics; protect player welfare clauses.

prompts.title: Tournament Run-of-Show (LAN Event)
prompts.prompt: Act as a tournament operations manager at Skyesports / NODWIN. Build a run-of-show for a 3-day Valorant LAN event in Bengaluru (16 teams, 800-seat venue, ₹50L prize). Cover: (1) day-by-day match schedule (group stage + playoffs + grand final), (2) AV stack (production, casting desk, broadcast TV), (3) team logistics (boarding, transport, dietary), (4) talent + caster roster, (5) crowd management + COVID-residual protocols, (6) media + press box, (7) broadcast partner SLA, (8) sponsor branding placement, (9) emergency + medical, (10) post-event tear-down. Constraints: align with Karnataka police + fire NOC; PPL/IPRS music license; INR INR cost cap ₹1.4 Cr; player welfare mandatory.

prompts.title: Player Performance Review (Quarterly)
prompts.prompt: Act as a coach + manager hybrid. Conduct a quarterly performance review for a roster of 5 BGMI players. For each player provide: (1) match data summary (KDA, ADR, headshot %, rotation efficiency), (2) tournament results contribution, (3) practice + scrim discipline (hours, attendance), (4) team-chemistry assessment (peer feedback), (5) social media discipline (no controversy), (6) physical + mental wellness (sleep, posture, screen-fatigue), (7) compensation review + INR top-up, (8) retention vs replace decision, (9) 90-day development plan. Constraints: respect player dignity; documented for any termination; sports-psychology consultation if needed; align with player contract.

prompts.title: Live Stream Production SOP
prompts.prompt: Act as a broadcast producer for esports. Build SOP for live-streaming our team's daily 6-hour scrim/practice on YouTube + Loco. Cover: (1) hardware (OBS, capture card, dual-PC, audio mixer), (2) graphics (lower-thirds, scoreboard, sponsor banner), (3) caster + analyst roles, (4) latency + bitrate (live 1080p60 + 6Mbps), (5) interaction (chat moderation, sub goals, donations), (6) ad-break placement (every 30 min), (7) clip-creation for socials, (8) DPDP-compliant data + recording disclosure. Constraints: align with YouTube + Loco TOS; PPL/IPRS for music; live-emoji moderation; archive 30-day; INR ad rate.

prompts.title: Player Contract Drafting
prompts.prompt: Act as a sports lawyer (Khaitan / J Sagar Esports practice). Draft a 2-year player contract template for an Indian esports org signing a 19-year-old BGMI pro. Cover: (1) compensation (monthly retainer ₹1.2L + tournament bonus + sponsorship share), (2) player obligations (practice, scrim, content), (3) IP (game-handle ownership, content rights), (4) social media policy, (5) NDA + non-compete (12-month post-exit, restricted to BGMI India), (6) termination (cause + without cause), (7) dispute resolution (arbitration in Bengaluru), (8) wellness + medical, (9) parental consent (since 19 — close to age boundary). Constraints: align with Indian Contract Act; respect player rights; transparent compensation; bilingual (English + Hindi).

prompts.title: Social Media Strategy for Org
prompts.prompt: Act as a social media manager for an Indian esports org (S8UL-style). Build a 30-day content calendar across Insta + YouTube + Twitter + Discord. Cover: (1) match days (live coverage, recaps, MVP features), (2) practice content (scrim highlights, behind-the-scenes), (3) lifestyle content (player vlogs, gaming setup tour, food + travel), (4) sponsor activations (with ASCI #ad), (5) community + memes, (6) tournament hype reels, (7) creator collaboration, (8) crisis-comms playbook (player controversy). Constraints: respect player privacy; bilingual (English + Hindi); alignment with sponsor brand safety; one weekly KPI review.

prompts.title: Bootcamp Schedule for Pre-Tournament
prompts.prompt: Act as a head coach for an Indian esports bootcamp. Design a 21-day bootcamp schedule for 5 players preparing for ESL India Championship (₹50L prize pool). Cover: (1) daily schedule (6 AM wake → 10 PM lights-out), (2) practice mix (mechanical drills 90 min, scrim 4 hrs, VOD review 90 min, theory 60 min), (3) physical training (gym 60 min daily, yoga, hand stretches), (4) nutrition (vegetarian + non-veg, 6 meals, INR ₹15K/player/month), (5) sleep discipline (8 hrs minimum), (6) mental health (psychologist 2x/week), (7) media training (1 hr/week), (8) game-day rituals + travel briefing. Constraints: respect players' weekly off; family contact allowed; mental wellness > performance.

prompts.title: Crisis Communication for Player Controversy
prompts.prompt: Act as a PR lead at an esports org. A star player tweeted offensive content about a community. Draft a 90-minute crisis-comms plan: (1) T+0-30 min: take down content, internal investigation, freeze player social, (2) T+30-60 min: meet player + family, draft public apology, secure legal counsel, (3) T+60-90 min: brand statement (org + player), suspend tournaments / sponsor obligations, internal team brief, (4) T+24 hrs: action announcement (suspension / fine / counselling), public dialogue with affected community, (5) T+30 days: review + reinstatement protocol. Constraints: align with ASCI + IT Rules; respect community pain; honour player due process; preserve org reputation; cite case-study (Indian + global precedents).

prompts.title: Esports Org Financial Model
prompts.prompt: Act as a CFO at an esports org. Build a 3-year financial model for an Indian org running 4 game divisions (BGMI, Valorant, CS:GO, FIFA — 26 players). Revenue: (1) sponsorship ₹6 Cr/year, (2) prize money ₹3 Cr, (3) content + streaming ₹2 Cr, (4) merchandising ₹1 Cr, (5) creator collabs ₹80L. Cost: (1) player salary ₹4 Cr, (2) coaches + staff ₹1.4 Cr, (3) bootcamp + travel ₹90L, (4) marketing ₹1.2 Cr, (5) infrastructure ₹40L. Cover: assumptions, sensitivities, break-even, runway, profit by year. Constraints: align with Indian GST / Companies Act; INR figures honest; investor-ready.

prompts.title: Coach Recruiting JD
prompts.prompt: Act as a Head of Performance. Write a 350-word JD for hiring a Head Coach for our BGMI team. Cover: (1) responsibilities (training plan, in-game-leader development, performance analytics, scrim partner network, tournament strategy), (2) requirements (5+ yrs competitive coaching, BGMI/PUBG meta deep, video-analysis fluent, English + Hindi), (3) bonus (sports psychology certification, prior LAN experience), (4) compensation ₹18-25L base + tournament bonus, (5) location (Bengaluru bootcamp, occasional travel), (6) work culture (non-toxic, growth mindset), (7) application + reference check. Constraints: align with Indian Contract Act; non-compete; respect work-hours culture (no abusive scrims); diverse hiring (women + LGBTQ+ welcome).

prompts.title: Streaming Revenue Optimization
prompts.prompt: Act as a content monetisation analyst. Optimise streaming revenue for a player with 320K subs on YouTube + Loco + 80K Twitch. Cover: (1) revenue mix (subs ₹X / mo, YouTube ad-rev ₹X, Loco gold ₹X, donations + super-chat ₹X, brand integration ₹X), (2) growth lever per channel (post-stream uploads, shorts, clips, collabs), (3) sponsor pipeline (3 deals at ₹2-5L each), (4) merch launch ₹6L year-1, (5) Patreon-style member tier, (6) UA optimisation for own merch + streams, (7) tax structuring (ITR-3, GST registration), (8) creative cadence (5x stream/week, 3x short/week). Constraints: ASCI compliance; INR transparent; protect work-life balance.

prompts.title: Event Sponsorship Activation Plan
prompts.prompt: Act as an event partnerships manager. Plan brand activation for ASUS ROG at our 3-day Skyesports LAN event. ASUS budget ₹40L. Cover: (1) brand booth (gaming PCs hands-on, photo-op), (2) jersey logo placement, (3) talent activation (caster wears ROG), (4) digital integration (live-stream lower-third, sponsor segment), (5) social co-promotion (joint Insta reel, contest, hashtag), (6) on-stage moment (MVP awarding by ASUS rep), (7) lead-capture mechanism (DPDP-compliant), (8) post-event ROI report. Constraints: ASCI #partner #ad; align with ASUS brand book; INR clear; deliverable contract; measurable impressions.

prompts.title: Roster Change Announcement
prompts.prompt: Act as a community manager at an esports org. Draft a 220-word roster-change announcement: 1 player exits (mutual decision), 1 player joins (free agent from rival team). Cover: (1) the departing player tribute (specific contributions + warm farewell + future-best-wishes), (2) the new player welcome (background, signing reason, role fit, jersey number), (3) team's path forward (no negative spin), (4) fans gratitude + community Q&A pointer, (5) 1 statement from coach + 1 from CEO. Constraints: tone classy, not corporate; respect both players; align with sports comms tradition; bilingual; tag both players.

prompts.title: Ladder + Ranking System Design
prompts.prompt: Act as a competitive game designer. Design a ranking + ladder system for a new Indian-built esports title. Cover: (1) tier hierarchy (Bronze - Silver - Gold - Platinum - Diamond - Master - Grandmaster), (2) MMR-based matchmaking (Glicko-2), (3) decay mechanism for inactive top-tier, (4) rank reset season cadence (3 months), (5) duo / squad rank mode rules, (6) anti-smurf detection (account behaviour ML), (7) ladder rewards (in-game cosmetic + INR ₹X for top-100), (8) appeal mechanism for matchmaking unfairness, (9) display + flex (toxic-prevention by capping rank-display visibility), (10) leaderboard public privacy (DPDP). Constraints: prevent toxicity at high ranks; respect time-poor players; align with fair-play SRO certification.
